title: ++CHRCH Access
body_html:
13. God Momentum (Part VI): Why We Keep Going When Times Are Hard

I remember when I was in high school... There was a Parisian girl that got transferred to our school one year.

Everyone was in awe with her. Perfect face. Cute clothes. And...

She was a musical prodigy.

Sophie played violin better than anyone I had ever seen.

She could even play Paganini caprice #24... AKA The Devil’s Song.

Seems her dad had put her through a vigorous Serena and Venus Williams disciplined type of musical upbringing.

It was weird to see someone with that much talent.

She always had her violin with her too. She called it “her wife” (or “ma femme”, as she would say in her cute little French accent).

Everytime the rest of us were hanging out... she went somewhere quiet to go practice.

Without fail.

Every day.

I was taking piano lessons at the time. Man, the only time I practiced was an hour before I had to go. I hated that shit.

But for her... That had been her lifestyle since she was four years old or something.

Well, one day after a long weekend, she came in with her arm in a cast.

She had gone skiing with her family and took a dive.

It wouldn't have been that big a deal for anyone else. I mean she WAS gonna be fine. Her cast would be off in as little as 2 months or something.

But she was MORTIFIED.

She couldn’t play the instrument she loved.

Despite that... After a couple of days, she started getting integrated into our circles.

She let the artists among us draw funny and rude stuff on her cast.

Her and I bonded one day at lunch on our love of Oreo cookies. Yeah, she splits them apart before she eats them too.

And on the weekends... We started seeing more of her now.

Even at the “illegal” beer drinking nights near the river.

But the craziest moment for me was seeing her walk to school one morning hand in hand with a new boyfriend. I had never seen this guy anywhere. He wasn't in our school.

I even felt a pang of jealousy.

Shit, I had never even considered asking her out myself. She had always seemed unattainable.

But it made sense... now that she didn’t have her "wife" anymore, there was a vacuum that could be filled.

When she finally got the cast off finally (in what seemed like overnight TBH) I knew that the honeymoon was over now.

I felt bad for the guy she had started dating.

Sophie was going to go back to practicing and that dude was about to get the shaft.

No more beer clinks and laughs with Sophie at our river parties.

No, It would be back to seeing her passionate head bobbing through the window of chemistry class as she practiced her scales at lunch.

But guess what?

That never happened.

She quit the violin instead.

She became like everyone else.

Years later I found out that she had married some guy who worked in the government and was raising kids.

I've never lost that moment of "awakening" that I received as a teenager.

I had seen firsthand that all it takes is for you to start sucking is to stop for just little while.

You can lose everything you gained over all those years of hard work... Overnight.

The vacuum will always get filled with something.

So if you’re trying to achieve greatness but you’re feeling down... Or diminished energy lately... Or feeling like you need some “self-care”...

Here’s the secret:

Don’t dare stop! Just go slower.

Do the absolute minimum you can handle every day, if you have to.

But keep going.

Take baby steps if you have to.

That's what will keep your excellence at top of mind.

It's still God Momentum when it's narrow.

It's still God Momentum when it's small.

You know when people accept their awards? What you're going through right now is the hard times they're talking about -- that they made it through. You will too.

Lean into this moment and it will get easier... Right there on the spot. Just watch!

Try it now.

Complete and Continue

    Lisa Wilson
    3 years ago
    Reply
    Link
    I found this relatable. I have been making money from my dancing for over a decade but now that my career dancing is over, it feels like my wife has died. I don't want to touch it, and haven't outside of work for 2 months now. I'm afraid of what my future would look like without her there. I've lost her many times and won her back so I know this doesn't have to be the end. Part of me is afraid that I sort of manifested this by choosing to get into corporate design work instead of following my dream to have my own studio. Is ux design my government husband? Kill me. The slow death of my club career is helping me see I haven't had dance as an art in my life for quite some time and have given most of it towards other's growth or for a paycheck. After starting this course, I realize I wan't lazy all those years, I just didn't have rituals, and that I was also severely sleep deprived and surrounded by haters. My art is going to turn into something else for me, hopefully in a way where I get to make art on my own terms from it and maybe do the one thing that I loved to do with it the most - teach in a space that not only facilitates the art, but is the art. Tomorrow I'm going to get my ass kicked in pilates and then come home for at least 15 minutes of dancing.

        Pradeep Pandey

        Chilling... when I met my music teacher, he was in his forties, recently back into music himself. He seemed like a happy bubbly guy but I sensed a deep sadness lying underneath in fleeting moments. One day he told me his story about how he used to be the best singer in his school, how he had one of the top artists producing his big single when he was 22, just ready to release but then sth happened and he had to go back home to support his family and music basically took a backseat. He showed me his photos and stuff and man he would've been at least a B-list star musician in our country back then. He knew that too. But he had to go for a few months and next thing you know 20 years have passed away already. Reminds me of that lyric from Pink Floyd's time "And then one day you find, ten years have got behind you, no one told you when to run, you missed the starting gun". Chilling. Absolutely chilling.

                        JT Kim
                        4 years ago
                        Reply
                        Link
                        I raised the bar today. It was 3 am. Malaa had just played the craziest and probably most intimate set I will see him play. I texted a girl friend who was there "lmk when you get home" and she responded "I'm at the after party". If it wasn't Friday morning then maybe but I responded "no thanks xD I gotta make music at 730". I get to the apartment thinking I'm gonna sleep for 4 hours, wake up with low energy and create for 15 minutes I couldn't do that though because FL Studio and Splice were left open and displayed on my computer for me as I enter my studio. So I thought, if I just create now then I can sleep longer. So I sit down and devote 15 minutes to make 2-3 sections. I couldn't stop though. I spent 15 minutes on each section designing each sound. I never spent so much effort or time on sound design at this stage of music production. After an hour and a half (I think I'm supposed to stop at one hour but I just couldn't stop), I had 6 distinguishable 8-bar segments which filled up 16 track channels between them. The track sounds like something Malaa would play (@ me Night Bass). TLDR: I raised the bar! Instead of just creating 2-3 segments of music, I created 6 while craftfully desgining the sound on Day 22 of my transformation.

                                          I'm surrounded by a lot of people in business in their first 1-3 years (myself included) and since day one I've been on the hustle train grinding. I've only realised recently how much I've been getting sucked into their vortex this past year of "you can work x amount of hours and earn x" or "why do you work so much?" or " you should create a passive income with x strategy" or "charge more" blah blah... Working through God Momentum is giving me a big fucking permission slip! I've never wanted to be mediocre, and I love what I do so why can't I live and breathe it? Thank you Father Bronques since starting this I'm shutting out that noise and tuning into this frequency.

                                                    This is exactly what I needed to read. I was just over finishing week 2 of the 21 day challenge when I fell jogging in the street. Busted up my knees and elbow sooo bad. I kept my “lights out” and wake up time the same, and did my daily practice but not much else while I was healing. I was feeling so bad about myself until I read this! I can make it through this, if just slowing down for a bit. Thanks, as always this is right on time.
